name: HEALTHY NAIL?
title: HEALTHY NAIL?
concern: A healthy nail is firm yet flexible, and can absorb normal shocks without splitting or chipping. Its surface is smooth with a subtle sheen and doesn't peel or flake at the tip.
solution: Even healthy nails benefit from proper nail care and protection during a manicure. Apply Mavala 002 Protective Base Coat before your nail polish to shield the nail plate and extend the life of your colour. Finish with Colorfix top coat over dry polish for added protection against chipping and flaking. For fast drying, use Mavadry Spray for a quick-dry finish, or Oil Seal Dryer for a moisturising treatment that nourishes cuticles while setting your manicure.

name: DRY NAIL?
title: DRY NAIL?
concern: A dry nail breaks and flakes. This type of nail has to be nourished and reinforced. The problem is frequently due to insufficient nutrients in the diet, but can also be caused by contact with harsh substances such as detergents.
solution: Dry, flaking nails require deep nourishment and a protective moisture barrier. Massage Nailactan nutritive cream into the nail base each night to deliver essential proteins and vitamins that restore flexibility and strength. Before applying nail polish, use Hydra-Base Coat to seal in moisture and protect the nail plate from further dehydration caused by detergents and harsh substances. Apply Mava-Flex serum twice a week for deep hydration, and finish with Colorfix top coat for lasting protection.

name: SOFT NAIL?
title: SOFT NAIL?
concern: A soft nail bends and tears easily, and needs extra protection. The cause of this problem is often a lack of proper vitamins and minerals in the daily diet. It can also be caused by illness.
solution: Soft nails that bend and tear easily need targeted nail strengthening treatment from within. Apply Mavala Scientifique K+ to the nail tips to harden and fortify the free edge. Use Mava-Strong fortifying base coat before your manicure to reinforce and protect the nail plate. Massage Nailactan nourishing cream into the nail base each night to stimulate the growth of stronger, healthier nails.

name: THIN NAIL?
title: THIN NAIL?
concern: A thin nail is subject to breaking and splitting, and should be reinforced. It is usually caused by dietary deficiencies of calcium, minerals and vitamins, but also can be brought on by illness.
solution: Thin nails prone to breaking and splitting need intensive nourishment to rebuild their strength. Use Mava-Strong fortifying base coat before your manicure to reinforce the nail plate and protect against splitting. Apply Nailactan nutritive cream to the nail base each night to provide the essential proteins and vitamins required for healthy nail growth. Finish with Gel Finish Top Coat for a thicker, more protective layer that adds strength and shine.

name: DAMAGED, BRITTLE NAIL?
title: Damaged, brittle nail?
concern: The nail lackes sheen, elasticity and resistance to impact. It is frequently due to insufficient nutrients in the diet, but also can be caused by contact with harsh substances such as detergents.
solution: Brittle nails that lack elasticity and sheen require a combined nail repair approach. Massage Nailactan cream into the nail base each night to counteract dryness and restore suppleness by delivering essential nourishing elements to the nail roots. For daily protection during manicures, apply Hydra-Base Coat as a moisturising base to shield damaged nails from further breakage, and finish with Colorfix top coat for added protection.

name: LIGHTLY RIDGED NAIL?
title: lightly ridged nail?
concern: A ridged nail has surface irregularities, usually in the form of ridges which run the length of the nail from the matrix to the tip. This condition has been linked to various dermatological disorders as well as illness.
solution: Ridged nails with surface irregularities benefit from consistent nourishment at the nail root to promote smoother growth. Apply Nailactan treatment cream to the nail base each night to deliver the nutrients needed for healthier nail development. Its rich formula supports proper keratin formation, gradually improving the appearance of ridged nails over time.

name: SPLITTING, BREAKING NAIL?
title: Splitting, breaking nail?
concern: Nails with fragile tips can easily split or break off. It is often due to dietary deficiencies or overexposure to water and strong cleaning substances.
solution: Nails with fragile tips that split or break easily need both strengthening and nourishment. Apply Mavala Scientifique K+ to the nail tips to harden and fortify the free edge. Use Mava-Strong fortifying base coat before your manicure to reinforce and protect the nail plate against splitting and breakage. Complement this with Nailactan nourishing cream, massaged into the nail base each night, to improve suppleness and elasticity from the root for lasting nail repair.

name: BITTEN NAIL?
title: Bitten nail?
concern: Nail biting is an uncontrollable reflex, frequently originating in childhood and is a reflection of nervousness. It usually occurs during a particular situation such as waiting at a red light, talking on the phone, during a moment of reflexion, when looking for an answer, etc.
solution: Breaking the nail biting habit starts with Mavala Stop, a bitter-tasting treatment applied to the nails that discourages biting through its unpleasant taste. Once nails begin to grow, apply Nail Shield to mechanically reinforce the nail with nylon fibres, creating a protective lattice that strengthens like a second nail. Use Mavaderma nutritive oil on the nail base and cuticle area daily to stimulate blood circulation and promote healthy nail growth.

name: SLOW GROWING NAIL?
title: SLow growing nail?
concern: The nail takes longer to reach its mature length; therefore, any damage or irregularity is longer lasting. This condition can be hereditary or brought on by ill-health or improper diet.
solution: Slow-growing nails need stimulation at the nail matrix to encourage faster, healthier growth. Apply Mavaderma nutritive oil to the nail base and cuticle area daily, massaging gently to stimulate blood circulation and promote nail growth. Its nourishing formula provides essential nutrients that support the development of strong, healthy nails over time.

name: LIGHTLY STAINED OR YELLOW NAIL?
title: lightly stained or yellow nail?
concern: Nails are generally yellow or stained due to tobacco or the application of nail polish directly onto the nail plate, without a protective base coat.
solution: Nail staining and yellowing is commonly caused by applying nail polish directly to the nail plate without adequate protection. Prevent further discolouration by always applying Mavala 002 Protective Base Coat before your manicure. This double-action base coat creates a protective barrier between the nail and coloured pigments, keeping nails clear and healthy while extending the life of your nail colour.

name: YELLOW, STAINED, DULL NAIL?
title: yellow, stained, dull nail?
concern: Yellow, stained, dull nails offer an unsightly aspect. Besides, this condition can affect the colour of your nail polish, turning it into a dirty beige, a yellow or a dull red, when applied. This phenomenon is generally due to tobacco or the application of nail polish directly onto the nail plate, without a base coat.
solution: Yellow, stained nails that lack lustre require deep nourishment at the nail root to promote the growth of clear, healthy nails. Massage Nailactan treatment cream into the nail base each night to deliver essential nutrients that support healthy nail renewal. With consistent use, new nail growth will gradually replace the discoloured portion, restoring a natural, healthy appearance.

name: OVERGROWN CUTICLES?
title: overgrown cuticles?
concern: Overgrown cuticles detract from the neat outline of the nail, and can split to form annoying hangnails. Excessive growth of the cuticle can be systematic, but it is more frequently caused by a lack of attention to regular moisturizing and removal of excess tissue.
solution: Overgrown cuticles can be softened and managed with a regular cuticle care routine. Apply Cuticle Remover to dissolve excess cuticle tissue, making it easy to gently push back without damage. Follow with Cuticle Cream to soften and nourish, and Cuticle Oil to moisturise the cuticle area, preventing dryness and hangnails while keeping the nail contour neat and healthy.

name: THICK, INFLEXIBLE NAIL?
title: thick, inflexible nails?
concern: This condition occurs because the keratin has become impregnated with calcium or silicon. This type of nail is often associated with persons suffering from arteriosclerosis. Frequent hard manual labour can also produce excessive keratinization and thick nails.
solution: Thick, inflexible nails with excessive keratinisation require targeted moisturising nail treatment. Brush Mava-Flex moisturising serum onto the entire nail surface twice a week, massage in, and leave to penetrate overnight for deep hydration. For your manicure routine, apply Hydra-Base Coat to hydrate and protect the nail plate, and finish with Colorfix top coat for additional protection against flaking. Use Oil Seal Dryer to fast dry your manicure without adding extra thickness to already thick nails.

name: NAIL WITH LONGITUDINAL GROOVES?
title: nail with longitudinal grooves?
concern: Longitudinal grooves are parallel furrows running the length of the nail which, when they reach the free edge, can crack and give an irregular appearance. They frequently appear in older people and can be evidence of certain illnesses such as lichen planus, renal failure, etc.
solution: Longitudinal grooves are parallel furrows that run the length of the nail and may crack at the free edge, giving an irregular appearance. Apply Nailactan treatment cream to the nail base each night to nourish the nail root and promote the growth of smoother, healthier nails. If grooves persist or worsen, we recommend consulting a dermatologist for a complete diagnosis and appropriate treatment plan.

name: NAIL WITH TRANSVERSAL GROOVES?
title: nail with transversal grooves?
concern: This condition can be caused by severe infection which harms the matrix and shows up as a deep groove running across the nail, which grows out with time. In certain chronic conditions, such as eczema, "manicure trauma", etc. waves may appear, separated by expanses of normal nail.
solution: Transversal grooves are horizontal ridges that can result from trauma or illness affecting the nail matrix. Massage Nailactan treatment cream into the nail base each night to support healthy nail renewal and promote smoother growth from the root. If the condition persists, we recommend consulting a dermatologist for a thorough diagnosis and treatment plan.

name: NAIL WITH SPOTTED EROSION?
title: nail with spotted erosion?
concern: Spotted erosion consists in small depressions on the nail surface, varying in number, size and placement, and are frequently associated with skin disorders.
solution: Spotted erosion appears as small depressions on the nail surface, often associated with skin conditions or minor trauma. Apply Nailactan nourishing cream to the nail base each night to support the growth of healthier, smoother nails from the root. For persistent or worsening cases, we recommend consulting a dermatologist for a complete diagnosis and appropriate nail treatment.

name: WHITE OR WHITE SPOTTED NAIL?
title: white or white spotted nails?
concern: Leuconychia (from the Greek leukos=white) can be due to a blow to the nail plate, heredity factors, or over-zealous manicures. Internal causes can be linked to menstruation or severe illness.
solution: White spots on nails (leuconychia) can result from minor trauma to the nail plate and typically grow out with time. Massage Nailactan nutritive cream into the nail base each night to support healthy nail renewal. Apply Mavala 002 Protective Base Coat for nail protection, followed by your chosen nail colour, and finish with Colorfix top coat to extend the life of your manicure and add a lasting shine.

name: BLACK OR BROWN NAIL?
title: BROWN OR BLACK NAIL?
concern: This problem frequently results from professional hazards, i.e. hair-dressers, carpenters, bakers, and photographers who come in contact with damaging products such as potassium permanganate, silver nitrate, abusive cosmetics (nail enamel containing many pigments), hair dyes, tobacco, etc. It can also be caused by general illness, mycosis infections, etc.
solution: Brown or black nail discolouration can result from contact with damaging substances or may indicate an underlying condition requiring medical attention. If caused by illness, we recommend consulting a dermatologist for proper diagnosis and treatment. For cosmetic coverage, apply Mavala 002 Protective Base Coat to shield the nail, followed by your preferred nail colour, and finish with Colorfix top coat for lasting protection and shine.

name: NAIL FUNGUS?
title: NAIL FUNGUS?
concern: Nail fungus (onychomycosis) is a common fungal infection that affects the nail plate, causing discoloration, thickening, and brittleness. The nail may turn yellow, brown, or white, and can become crumbly or distorted over time. Fungal infections thrive in warm, moist environments and can spread from one nail to another. They are often contracted in public places like swimming pools, gyms, or through shared footwear. If left untreated, the infection can worsen and become painful.
solution: MAVA-MED is a powerful antifungal nail solution specifically formulated to tackle fungal nail infections effectively. Its unique "Instant Killer" complex stops fungi from spreading and aids in nail healing. Enriched with lavender and eucalyptus essential oils, lactic acid and urea, it creates an environment that deters fungal growth while nourishing the nail plate. Apply MAVA-MED twice daily to the affected nails after cleaning and drying them thoroughly. Continue treatment for several weeks until the infection clears and healthy nail growth is visible. For severe or persistent infections, we recommend consulting a dermatologist for a complete diagnosis.
